events.title: Pawsitively Pets: Hands-on Animal Education
events.description: Pawsitively Pets is a hands-on animal education program for children of all ages. Our wonderful animal team is made up of an assortment of rescued pets that have found their forever home with us. Our goal is to provide our animal team with a permanent home and the best possible care, while sharing our love or animals with others. All programs focus on responsible pet ownership, and the life-long commitment of owning a pet. Please note this program is suitable for children 5 years old up with adu

events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Together Time
events.description: This is a program for parents and caregivers with children from birth - 6 years. Families have the opportunity to socialize while children develop new skills in an early learning environment.

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.  March Break Challenge: Build a mall as a group from 2-3pm If you identify as a person with a disability and require an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services by email, accessibleservices@tpl.ca, or voicemail, 416-393-7099, to make a request. Please contact us at le

events.title: Baby Time
events.description: Bouncing and tickling rhymes, stories, and songs for babies from birth to 18 months with their parents or caregivers.

events.title: Baby and Toddler Story Time
events.description: Join us for bouncing rhymes, songs and stories in this program for babies, birth to 3 years, and their parents or caregivers. Baby and Toddler Story Time runs from 10:00 to 10:30. Children, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play, from 10:30 to 11:00.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for rhymes, songs and stories for children from 0-5, with their parents or caregivers. A fun activity, game or craft to follow. Children, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and rhymes in this program for children ages 5 years and under, and their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 10:30 to 11:00 am. Stay and Play from 11:00 to 11:30 am Drop-in. No registration required.

events.title: Game on! Board Game Playtime
events.description: Join us for a fun-filled day of board games and puzzles! Play your favourites or try something new! Brings your friends and family or meet new people at the library. Games available for use in the Children's Area. Available games include: Ball Fall Jumbo (Ages 3+), STEM Pretend Flower Garden Toy Set (Ages 3-6), Operation (Ages 6+), STEM Road Builder Game (Ages 4-8), Dinosaur Tetra Tower Balance Stacking Blocks Game (Ages 6+) Available puzzles include: Giant World Map Puzzle (

events.title: Crafturday
events.description: Join us for a day of fun and crafts. Partake in a new and engaging craft every Saturday from 9:30am - 4:30pm. Drop-in and self-serve. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. If your child lives with a disability, and requires an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services at accessibleservices@tpl.ca or phone 416-393-7099. Please contact us at least three weeks in advance.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers.  Drop-in, registration is not required. This is a year-round program.

events.title: Family Time

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for a fun storytime with stories, songs and rhymes followed by a craft or activity. For children ages 5 and under with their parents or caregivers.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers. events.title: Builder's Club
events.description: Join us on Saturday mornings and build to your heart’s content!  LEGO, DUPLO, and KEVA planks will be provided for you to explore your creativity. All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Sunday Crafternoon
events.description: Join us for Sunday crafts. Different activity each week. Sundays 12-4:30 pm. Drop-in.

events.title: EarlyON Family Resource Program
events.description: Learn about quality community resources for young families, enjoy a circle time for the young ones, and get some nutritious snacks. events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

events.title: Henna Art Workshop for Children
events.description: Celebrate Asian Heritage Month and discover the art of henna, decorative designs which are created on a person's body, using a paste of natural organic henna. Observe and enjoy the henna application and learn about its history, recipe and traditions. Led by Henna Artist, Hina Yasir. Drop In. Space is limited. Please Note: Henna will be available on a first-come, first-served basis. The final henna application will begin no later than 2:55 pm. No new designs will be started after this time.

This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration necessary.

events.title: Painting the Seasons - A Pipa Solo Concert
events.description: Audience will have this wonderful and rare opportunity to appreciate the unique Chinese music instrument live performance. Internationally renowned Toronto-based Pipa artist Wen Zhao will perform a solo concert celebrating China’s rich musical legacy, with a traditional and folk music repertoire spanning across dynasties. Open to all ages. No registration required, limited to a room capacity.

This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing and how to use the Prusa software. Registration will start 2 weeks before this class.

events.title: History of Generative Music: The Early Modern Era
events.description: The twentieth century brought radical new ideas about what music could be - from compositions determined by coin tosses to pieces that rearranged themselves with every performance. events.title: Steelpan: Hands On Fun!
events.description: Musician Suzette Vidale leads participants in a hands-on experience with the steelpan! ​Have fun while learning about the instrument, the proper technique of holding sticks, stance and playing a scale. Suzette will teach everyone a simple song for participants to play. No musical knowledge required! This session is for adult participants. Mature teens are welcome. Younger participants can join the 3:30pm session. Please register for a spot. Information Collection Notice: The personal inform

events.title: Saturday Knit & Crochet Circle
events.description: Want to learn how to knit or crochet with a fun group of people? Looking for help with your project? You can also share your projects with others, teach or learn a technique, and enjoy great conversation. All levels of expertise are welcome. Bring your own yarn, needles, and crochet hooks. All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration necessary.

events.title: From the Golden Age to the Modern Era: A Musical Odyssey of East Asia
events.description: This concert introduces the history of the yangqin and its diverse regional and folk traditions, guiding the audience on a musical journey across China through sound, storytelling, and performance. This performance will include selections from different eras and styles, along with brief introductions to provide an overview of the musical journey. events.description: Are you looking for a quiet space to study after school? Join our quiet study hall at Locke's program room every Thursday for individual or group study. events.title: Youth Hub: Boba Drink Making with Chef Yumiko
events.description: Chef Yumiko leads a hands-on workshop to make the popular Asian beverage, Brown Sugar Milk with Boba. Participants will learn to prepare and use the classic ingredients including milk, molasses and tapioca pearls to make this visually pleasing and sweet drink. Chef Yumiko will also provide a brief history of Boba and why it's so popular globally. Food allergy notice: Please be aware that food will be used in this program. events.title: Indigenous Film Screening - The Stand
events.description: On a misty morning in the fall of 1985, a small group of Haida people blockaded a muddy dirt road on Lyell Island, demanding the government work with Indigenous people to find a way to protect the land and the future. In a riveting new feature documentary drawn from more than a hundred hours of archival footage and audio, award-winning director Christopher Auchter ( Now Is the Time ) recreates the critical moment when the Haida Nation’s resolute act of vision and conscience changed the world. In

events.title: Toronto Games Week: An Intro to DaggerHeart
events.description: In celebration of Toronto Games Week (June 11 - Jun 17) come learn the ins and outs of the Tabletop Roleplaying phenomenon known as DaggerHeart. Players will fall into the roles of our heroic adventurers and prove they have the strength, skills, knowledge and bravery to become the Games Week Champions! events.title: Throwback Thursdays: Great Movies on the Big Screen
events.description: Come join us for a radical retro rewind projected on the big screen. Whether you're a first-time viewer or long-time fan, these movies are sure to please. April 16: Meet the Parents (2000, PG) April 23: Rounders (1998, R) April 30: No Country for Old Men (2007, 18A) May 7: Apollo 13 (1995, PG) May 14: The Martian (2015, PG) * special start time! 5:30 pm May 21: Contact (1997, PG) * special start time! 5:30 pm May 28: Star Trek (2009, PG) All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Silent Book Club

events.title: Create Pop Music with AI Tools for Teens
events.description: Have you ever had a melody stuck in your head and wished you could turn it into a real song? AI tools are making it easier than ever to bring your musical experiences to life - but how do you make sure the music you create is truly yours? Explore how to use AI as a creative assistant to build radio-ready pop tracks based on your own original ideas. events.title: How to Become a Volunteer
events.description: Interested in learning more about volunteering opportunities in Toronto? This session is intended for the volunteer seekers who are new to volunteering, or in need to understand the process of formal volunteering. Join us to learn how and where to search for opportunities that best match your interests and what to expect after you apply for a volunteer role. This session caters to all demographics. Presented by Volunteer Toronto .

Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Games
events.description: Drop in to the Youth Hub every Tuesday and Thursday to play video games. Play with friends, by yourself, or maybe someone new.  Tuesdays will be Nintendo Switch days and Thursdays will be PS4 days.  *NEW* Starting at 3:30 pm, teens can sign up for a time slot to use the consoles. If there is no demand, gaming will be awarded on a first come first serve basis.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, wher

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Youth Hub:  Nail Night
events.description: Unleash your creativity as you design your own stunning nail art! Join us for one or all of our fabulous Nail Nights on the first Monday of every month. Drop-in, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Soundscape
events.description: Next we will be inspired by the library’s architecture and happenings as we are guided by musician Aniqa Qadir to create a soundscape. Working in groups, visitors will be prompted to absorb the sounds of the building and led through the process of creating an audio journey. This playful session welcomes participants to break the whisper norm of a library and interrupt the space with their observations. Led by Aniqa Qadir and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. This year we are reading:  February 19 -  The Strangers by Katherena Vermette April 16 -  Big Little Lies by Liane Moriarty June 18 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 17 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 19 -  Small Things Like These by

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around the Branch Tour
events.description: The Brookbanks branch and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry team to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Free program.  Registration will open up one month before the program date. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Presented as part of TPL’s environmental program series, Our Fragile P

Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.

events.title: Youth Hub:  Nail Night
events.description: Unleash your creativity as you design your own stunning nail art! Join us for one or all of our fabulous Nail Nights on the first Monday of every month. Drop-in, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.
